name: Safe synthetic playbook: Phishing message interaction (lab)
description: Structured lab simulation of a user acknowledging a suspicious message and a benign redirect to a lab-designated host. No mail APIs, no harvested credentials, no weaponized content.

simulated_timeline.synthetic_narrative: User reports a suspicious external message using the lab reporting control.
Simulator correlates the report to a benign lab landing page hostname (non-routable example domain).
Awareness and decision-engine stubs queue nudges and a low-severity case tag without blocking production mail.

events_emitted.headline: Awareness/reporting alert recorded
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Synthetic SOC envelope for a voluntary user report; no mailbox compromise assumed.

events_emitted.headline: Synthetic finding correlated to identity
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Classifier tags the engagement as simulated social-engineering context for policy evaluation.

expected_agents_triggered.triggered_reason_synthetic: User-reported phishing context requires awareness routing in the lab.
Synthetic alert opens a low-severity incident track for metrics only.

expected_remediation.summary: Simulated nudge to training, policy blocklist update for lab host, optional case tag; no production quarantine in lab mode.

expected_autonomous_remediation.synthetic_action: Attach lab training module and record decision-engine evaluation row (stub).
Append hostname to denylist in policy simulation namespace only.

expected_human_approval_gates.synthetic_rationale: Operator confirms whether production-style quarantine rules should be mirrored in the tenant.

expected_final_report.title: Phishing acknowledgment — synthetic playbook outcome
expected_final_report.synthetic_executive_summary: User-driven report was correlated to a benign lab URL; residual risk tracked as awareness and policy posture, not inbox compromise.
